What is the purpose of Python's 'GIL' (Global Interpreter Lock)?

options.A: To encrypt source code to prevent unauthorized reverse engineering.
options.B: To ensure only one thread executes Python bytecode at a time, making CPython's memory management safe.
options.C: To lock nested loops from causing unexpected endless execution.
options.D: To protect private class variables from being modified outside the module.

What is the primary difference between a shallow copy and a deep copy of a nested list?

options.A: Shallow copy copies the top-level list structure but shares references to the nested lists; deep copy copies everything recursively.
options.B: Shallow copy converts elements to strings; deep copy keeps them as numbers.
options.C: Deep copy can only be performed on tuples, while shallow copy works on lists.
options.D: There is no difference; they behave identically in modern Python versions.
